Luma.sentiment: Overwhelmingly warm. Audiences keep returning to three things: the film speaks to real childhood fear without talking down, adults see their own anxiety in it, and the single-light-in-the-dark imagery is instantly iconic.

loves.title: It takes kids' fear seriously
loves.quote: My daughter is terrified of the dark and she walked out of the screening asking for a firefly nightlight.

angles.hook: Bring the kid who still checks under the bed.
angles.primary_text: Luma is scared of the dark too. This summer she becomes the light her whole swarm needs. In cinemas this summer.
angles.visual: Midnight field, one warm lamplight glow bottom-left, tiny firefly silhouette, headline set large in rounded display.

angles.primary_text: A film about being scared of the dark, for everyone who ever was. Family tickets at participating cinemas.
angles.visual: A child's bedside lamp glowing against deep blue, constellation motif above, wordmark small in the corner.

loves.title: Adults see their own anxiety in it
loves.quote: Went in expecting a kids film about bugs. Got a film about my own anxiety.

angles.hook: You were never really afraid of the dark.
angles.primary_text: You were afraid of being the only light. Luma understands. This summer, on the big screen.
angles.visual: Vast dark field, one small glowing firefly centre frame, enormous negative space, headline top-third.

loves.title: The imagery already feels iconic
loves.quote: That single warm light drifting over the dark field is going to be on posters forever.

angles.hook: One small light. One long night.
angles.primary_text: From the story room: every frame of the harvest-night sequence was lit by a single source. See why on the big screen.
angles.visual: Poster-pure: flat midnight block, one lamplight circle, tall grass silhouettes along the bottom edge.

The Deep End.sentiment: Very positive. The comedy lands hard with kids while the masking theme hits teens and adults unexpectedly deep; the ink-cloud gag and the coach are already quotable.

loves.title: Shy kids feel seen
loves.quote: Every shy teenager needs to see this. The swim meet finale where he stops imitating is perfect.

angles.hook: He can imitate anyone. Except himself.
angles.primary_text: The Deep End, a comedy about the hardest stroke there is: being yourself. In cinemas this autumn.
angles.visual: Pool-lane blues, small octopus silhouette mid-lane, caustic light ripples, headline in rounded display.

loves.title: The comedy genuinely lands
loves.quote: The ink cloud gag never got old, and my 9 year old has quoted the coach every day since.

angles.primary_text: The funniest thing in the pool this autumn. Bring the whole lane.
angles.visual: Sky-blue field, playful ink-splat shape in deep navy, goggles motif, one-line headline.

Wrench & Rivet.sentiment: Warm with one caveat. The two-robot chemistry and the fix-it heart are the draw; the city-hall subplot is the only recurring gripe, so marketing should stay on the buddies and the junkyard.

loves.title: The buddy chemistry carries it
loves.quote: Every scene with the two robots alone together is gold though.

angles.hook: Two stubborn robots. One junkyard. Zero compromise.
angles.primary_text: Wrench & Rivet inherit the same scrap heap this spring. Sparks, obviously, fly.
angles.visual: Warm rust-orange field, two mismatched robot silhouettes back to back, tool motifs scattered small.

loves.title: People who fix things feel honoured
loves.quote: My dad fixes cars and he said it's the first film that gets why people love repairing things.

angles.hook: For everyone who fixes what others throw away.
angles.primary_text: A love letter to repair, one bolt at a time. In cinemas this spring.
angles.visual: A single sunflower growing through flat scrap silhouettes, ember accent, wide negative space.

Paper Moon.sentiment: Quietly devastating in the best way. Sparse feedback so far, but every note is about calling a grandparent afterwards; the kitchen-table lamplight scenes are the emotional core.

loves.title: It sends people straight to the phone
loves.quote: Call your grandmother after this one.

angles.primary_text: Paper Moon opens this winter. Bring someone who taught you something with their hands.
angles.visual: Origami crane silhouette against a huge paper-white moon on midnight, lamplight window glow lower right.
